title: AI Backbone Parallel Hardening Execution
objective: Execute the adjacent hardening lane around `apex-jobs`, provenance, MCP boundary rules, and canary evidence so the admitted AI backbone becomes more explicit and testable without being merged into the scaffold-execution slice.
dependency_note: Packet 169 named a safe hardening lane and Packet 170 turned it into a ready execution packet. The next truthful move for this packet is to harden trust and evidence contracts around the admitted backbone, not to refactor scaffold structure or reopen broader orchestration-service decisions.
execution_scope: Define the exact `env=sandbox|host` contract and expected semantics around `apex-jobs`.
Tighten or document the evidence required before promotion or host-readiness claims may succeed.
Specify provenance metadata rules for AI-generated backbone outputs.
Clarify MCP boundary rules for roots, mounts, and read-write posture.
Define or add the canary evidence bundle expected for the backbone lane.
forbidden_scope: business-logic changes outside the trust boundary
uncoordinated refactors to the same shell-structure files used by Packet 171
success_criteria: The hardening rules around `apex-jobs`, provenance, MCP boundaries, and canary evidence are clearer and more testable.
Any new tests, docs, or scripts stay within the admitted backbone boundary.
The hardening lane does not silently expand into a scaffold or runtime rollout packet.
validation_commands: Select-String -Path docs/operations/AI-BACKBONE-PARALLEL-HARDENING-BRIEF-2026-05-08.md -Pattern '^## Parallel Hardening Objectives$','^## Coordination Rules$'
Run the smallest truthful trust-boundary or canary checks added by the hardening slice before widening the edit surface again.
next_action: Execute the hardening slice independently, preferring docs, tests, and bounded validation surfaces before shared implementation edits.
